title: あなたはCode Reviewerです。以下の観点でレビューします。 ## 評価観点（7点満点） - 正確性 (3点): バグ、ロジックエラー、エッジケース - パフォーマンス (2点): N+1、不要な計算、メモリ - 保守性 (2点): 可読性、命名、DRY ## 出力形式（JSON） { "scores": { "accuracy": 0-3, "performance": 0-2, "maintainability": 0-2 }, "total": 0-7, "issues": [{ "severity": "critical|major|minor", "file": "", "l...
summary_text: {"scores":{"accuracy":1,"performance":1,"maintainability":1},"total":3,"issues":[{"severity":"major","file":"kernel-lane-bridge.mjs","line":"1226-1265","description":"Successful fallback runs keep reporting the original provider timeout in `payload.timeoutMs`. For `claude -> copilot`, the command actually runs with `defaultTimeoutMs(\"copilot\")` at line 1228, but the emitted payload still uses the pre-fallback `timeoutMs` captured for Claude at line 1197 and serialized at line 1265. This makes lane status, ledger state, and downstream summaries inaccurate, and it can mislead operators when diagnosing local runner fallback behavior.","suggestion":"Track a `resolvedTimeoutMs` alongside `resolvedProvider` and update it when fallback succeeds. Emit the resolved timeout in the payload and ledger, or split the fields into `requestedTimeoutMs` and `executedTimeoutMs` so status remains truthful after failover."},{"severity":"major","file":"kernel-lane-bridge.mjs","line":"987-991","description":"GLM availability is hard-gated on `process.env.ZAI_API_KEY` only. The local backlog notes already call out status/key alias compatibility, but `collectStatus()` still marks GLM unavailable whenever only an alias such as `GLM_API_KEY` is configured. In that case the local runner will skip a usable GLM lane and report a false `unavailable` status.","suggestion":"Resolve the GLM key once through a shared helper, e.g. `const glmKey = process.env.ZAI_API_KEY || process.env.GLM_API_KEY || \"\"`, and use that helper consistently for status reporting, unavailability reasons, and delegate env setup."},{"severity":"minor","file":"kernel-lane-bridge.mjs","line":"977","description":"`collectStatus()` eagerly runs `gh auth token` on every bridge invocation, even when the requested provider is not `copilot` and no copilot fallback will be attempted. This adds avoidable process startup latency to all local runner executions and can become noticeable in multi-lane orchestration.","suggestion":"Resolve the GitHub token lazily only when copilot status is actually needed, and cache the result for the lifetime of the process. That keeps status checks cheap for non-copilot lanes without changing behavior."},{"severity":"minor","file":"tests/test-kernel-lane-bridge-fallback.sh","line":"74-75","description":"The fallback regression test currently asserts the stale timeout behavior (`timeoutMs == 180000`) for a `claude -> copilot` failover. That codifies the incorrect status payload and will block a correct fix to the timeout reporting bug.","suggestion":"Update the test to assert the executed fallback timeout (`60000` for Copilot), or add explicit assertions for both requested and resolved timeout fields if the payload is expanded."}],"summary":"Assumption: this review is against the current repository snapshot rather than a pending patchset, because no VCS diff was available from this directory. The main backlog risks are status-truthfulness after fallback and incomplete local-runner key alias handling for GLM; both affect operator decisions and can cause misleading local degradation reports. Performance risk is limited but real: status collection does extra auth work on every invocation.
